disclaimer: SAMPLE DATA FOR DEMO PURPOSES ONLY. Not legal advice. In production, this database would be built with Lengea's proprietary state-by-state compliance data and reviewed by attorneys.

texas.name: Texas
texas.cpom_description: Texas enforces a strict Corporate Practice of Medicine (CPOM) doctrine. Non-physicians cannot directly own or control a medical practice that provides medical aesthetic services.

ownership.non_physician_path: MSO model — non-physician owns the Management Services Organization (LLC) which handles all business/admin functions. A separate physician-owned PLLC or PA handles all clinical services. The two entities are connected by a Management Services Agreement (MSA).
ownership.key_statute: Texas Occupations Code; Texas Medical Board Rule 193

medical_director.who_qualifies: Licensed physician (MD or DO) with active unrestricted Texas medical license
medical_director.supervision_level: Must establish written protocols, provide oversight, and be available for consultation. Does not need to be on-site at all times but must be reasonably available.
medical_director.geographic_restriction: No strict statutory geographic limit, but must be able to respond within a reasonable timeframe
key_duties: Establish and approve treatment protocols for all medical procedures
Supervise and delegate medical acts to qualified personnel
Conduct or ensure completion of good faith examinations
Review charts and ensure quality of care
Ensure all staff are properly trained and credentialed

can_perform: Supervise and delegate to qualified staff

nurse_practitioner_np.supervision_required: Must have prescriptive authority agreement with supervising physician. Cannot practice independently for med spa services.
nurse_practitioner_np.notes: NPs cannot own the medical practice entity. Can own the MSO.

physician_assistant_pa.supervision_required: Must have supervising physician. Written delegation required.
physician_assistant_pa.notes: PAs may hold minority ownership stake (up to 49%) in the medical entity.

can_perform: Injectables (Botox, fillers) under physician delegation and orders
registered_nurse_rn.supervision_required: Must work under physician-issued orders. Cannot independently initiate treatment.
registered_nurse_rn.notes: RN cannot diagnose, prescribe, or perform good faith exams. Must have patient-specific physician orders.

can_perform: Laser hair removal (in some cases with training)
aesthetician.supervision_required: Cannot perform medical procedures. Limited to non-medical cosmetic treatments.
aesthetician.notes: Cannot perform microneedling, injectables, or any procedure classified as practice of medicine.

key_regulations: Texas Medical Board Rule 193 governs delegation of medical acts
HB 3749 (effective Sept 1, 2025) — Texas IV Therapy Bill clarifies who can administer IV therapy and supervision requirements
HB 3567 proposals may affect RN supervision requirements — actively monitor
Advertising must comply with Texas Occupations Code Chapter 102 (Truth in Healthcare Marketing)
Good faith exams required before first treatment — can be virtual but phone-only not recommended for new patients
All medical spa services (injectables, laser, IV, microneedling) classified as practice of medicine

entity_formation.physician_entity: PLLC or PA filed with Texas Secretary of State
entity_formation.mso_entity: LLC filed with Texas Secretary of State
entity_formation.dba_filing: Assumed name certificate filed with county clerk

recent_developments: HB 3749 (2025): New IV therapy supervision standards
Increased Texas Medical Board enforcement actions against non-compliant med spas
Growing scrutiny of MSO structures — TMB looking for 'paper directors' who are uninvolved

california.name: California
california.cpom_description: California has one of the strictest CPOM doctrines in the nation. Business & Professions Code Section 2052 reserves medical practice ownership exclusively to licensed physicians. Regulators have aggressively shut down non-compliant spas.

ownership.non_physician_path: MSO (LLC) + Physician-owned PC (Professional Corporation). The 'Friendly PC-MSO' model is the gold standard. Physician must own at least 51% of the PC. Certain allied health professionals (NPs, PAs, RNs) may own up to 49% of the PC.
ownership.np_ownership: Minority ownership up to 49% of PC. Under Assembly Bill 890 (effective Jan 2023), NPs may gain expanded practice authority. Full independent NP med spa ownership expected to evolve by 2026-2027.
ownership.rn_ownership: Up to 49% of the PC. Cannot own majority.
ownership.key_statute: Business & Professions Code §2052; Medical Board of California enforcement

medical_director.who_qualifies: Licensed physician (MD or DO) with active unrestricted California medical license
medical_director.supervision_level: Direct supervision required for certain procedures. Written standardized procedures must be in place. Must be actively involved — California aggressively targets 'paper directors'.
medical_director.geographic_restriction: Must be able to respond in-person within a reasonable timeframe. Physical proximity expected.
key_duties: Maintain complete control over all clinical decisions
Establish written standardized procedures for all delegated acts
Supervise all clinical staff including nurses and injectors
Ensure all patient payments for medical services flow to the PC, not the MSO
Conduct or oversee good faith examinations

nurse_practitioner_np.supervision_required: Must practice under standardized procedures or physician supervision. AB 890 expanding scope gradually.
nurse_practitioner_np.notes: May own up to 49% of the PC. AB 890 timeline: group practice without standardized procedures after meeting criteria, full practice authority after 3 additional years.

physician_assistant_pa.supervision_required: Must have supervising physician. Written delegation agreement required.
physician_assistant_pa.notes: Delegation agreement must be on file. PA scope is broad but always under physician supervision.

can_perform: Injectables under standardized procedures and physician orders
registered_nurse_rn.supervision_required: Must work under physician orders and standardized procedures. Cannot independently initiate treatment.
registered_nurse_rn.notes: Cannot perform microneedling independently. Must have patient-specific orders.

aesthetician.supervision_required: Cannot perform any medical procedures. Strictly non-medical cosmetic treatments.
aesthetician.notes: Cannot perform microneedling (classified as medical in CA). Cannot perform laser treatments except laser hair removal in some cases.

key_regulations: B&P Code §2052 — Strict Corporate Practice of Medicine doctrine
Only licensed physicians may own a Professional Corporation (PC) providing medical services
MSO may never control or influence clinical decisions, staffing of clinical roles, or medical records
Assembly Bill 890 (2020, effective 2023) — expanding NP scope of practice gradually
Medical Board of California actively enforces against 'paper directors' and non-compliant MSO arrangements
Fee-splitting with non-physicians is prohibited — MSO fees must be at fair market value
Salon license required if offering facials or non-medical aesthetician services

entity_formation.physician_entity: PC (Professional Corporation) filed with California Secretary of State
entity_formation.mso_entity: LLC filed with California Secretary of State
entity_formation.dba_filing: Fictitious Business Name Statement filed with county clerk
entity_formation.estimated_timeline: 8-14 weeks (CA processing times are significantly longer than most states)
key_filings: PC Articles of Incorporation with CA SOS
LLC Articles of Organization for MSO

recent_developments: AB 890 NP scope expansion continuing to roll out — monitor for med spa impacts
People ex rel. Allstate v. Discovery Radiology (2023): Court held non-physician control over staffing/billing can constitute unlicensed practice
Medical Board increasing investigations of MSO arrangements
Processing times remain long — plan 3-4 months for full entity setup

florida.name: Florida
florida.cpom_description: Florida does NOT enforce a traditional Corporate Practice of Medicine doctrine for med spas. Non-physicians CAN directly own med spa businesses, making Florida one of the more permissive states. However, all medical services must still be overseen by a licensed physician medical director.

ownership.non_physician_path: Non-physicians can own a med spa directly as an LLC. An MSO structure is not required but may still be advisable for liability protection. A licensed physician medical director must oversee all medical services regardless of ownership structure.
ownership.rn_ownership: Technically possible but must have physician medical director oversight for all medical services
ownership.key_statute: F.S. §400.9935 (Health Care Clinic Act); F.S. §458.347 (PA supervision); F.S. §464.012 (APRN protocols)

medical_director.who_qualifies: Licensed physician (MD or DO) with active unrestricted Florida medical license. Board certification preferred but not always required.
medical_director.supervision_level: Must establish protocols and provide oversight. Does not need to be on-site at all times but must be available for consultation and provide active supervision.
medical_director.geographic_restriction: Should be within reasonable proximity to the practice. No strict statutory distance requirement.
key_duties: Ensure compliance with all applicable regulations
Verify licensure of all clinical staff
Establish and approve treatment protocols
Maintain adequate medical recordkeeping
Provide quality assurance oversight

nurse_practitioner_np.supervision_required: Non-autonomous APRNs: written physician protocol per F.S. §464.012. Autonomous APRNs under HB 607 (2020): may practice independently in primary care but NOT in aesthetics — physician protocols still required for med spa services.
nurse_practitioner_np.notes: Autonomous APRN status does NOT extend to aesthetic procedures. Physician protocols remain standard of care for med spa services.

physician_assistant_pa.supervision_required: Must practice under written supervision agreement with physician per F.S. §458.347. Agreement defines duties, prescriptive privileges, and supervision parameters.
physician_assistant_pa.notes: PAs can own med spas in Florida with a medical director.

can_perform: Injectables (Botox, fillers) under physician protocols and direct supervision
registered_nurse_rn.supervision_required: Must work under direct supervision of medical director. Physician must remain available for consultation.
registered_nurse_rn.notes: Florida Board of Nursing guidance on RN scope for injectables has been contradictory — consult legal counsel. Malpractice insurance strongly recommended.

aesthetician.notes: Cannot perform injectables, microneedling, or laser treatments.

key_regulations: Florida does NOT have strict CPOM — non-physicians can own med spas directly
Health Care Clinic Act (F.S. §400.9935) — clinic registration and medical director requirements
HB 607 (2020) — APRN autonomous practice authority, but does NOT extend to aesthetic/med spa procedures
F.S. §458.347 — PA supervision requirements
Board of Medicine has increased enforcement of med spa compliance
No mandatory chart review percentage — but documentation of physician oversight required
Good faith exams required before first treatment

entity_formation.physician_entity: LLC, PA, PLLC, or Corporation filed with Florida Division of Corporations
entity_formation.mso_entity: MSO optional — may be advisable for liability separation even though not required by CPOM
entity_formation.dba_filing: Fictitious Name Registration with FL Division of Corporations
key_filings: LLC or other entity formation with FL Division of Corporations

recent_developments: Continued clarity around APRN autonomous practice — NOT applicable to aesthetics
Increased Board of Medicine enforcement actions
Growing market — Florida is one of the top 3 states for med spa openings
No strict CPOM makes FL attractive for entrepreneurs, but proper structure still critical for liability

new_york.name: New York
new_york.cpom_description: New York enforces one of the most rigid interpretations of the Corporate Practice of Medicine doctrine. Education Law §6522 prohibits non-physicians from owning, controlling, or influencing medical practice. Med spas must operate through a physician-owned PC or PLLC authorized by the New York State Education Department (NYSED). Violations are a Class E felony.

ownership.non_physician_path: MSO (LLC) + Physician-owned PC or PLLC. The professional entity must be authorized by the New York State Education Department (NYSED). PLLC formation requires affidavit filing with NYSED Office of the Professions. MSO arrangements are closely scrutinized — cannot involve fee-splitting based on volume or value of business.
ownership.np_ownership: NPs may form a PC or PLLC after meeting 3,600 hours supervised practice requirement for Full Practice Authority under Education Law §6902(3)(b-1). Before that, must have written collaborative agreement with physician.
ownership.key_statute: Education Law §6522; Education Law §6530(19); Regents Rules Part 29

medical_director.who_qualifies: Licensed physician (MD or DO) with active unrestricted New York medical license verified through NYSED Office of the Professions
medical_director.supervision_level: Active involvement required — New York strictly prohibits 'name-only' medical directors. On-site supervision required for medical procedures. Must have training and experience relevant to procedures offered.
medical_director.geographic_restriction: Must be physically present or immediately available. Remote-only medical direction is insufficient.
key_duties: Direct supervision and oversight of all medical procedures
Establish written protocols for all delegated procedures
Conduct or oversee good faith examinations
Maintain complete medical records
Ensure all practitioners work within their licensed scope
Active involvement in clinical operations — not just a signature

can_perform: Injectables under physician orders or independent (with FPA)
nurse_practitioner_np.supervision_required: Under 3,600 hours: written collaborative agreement with physician required. After 3,600 hours: Full Practice Authority (FPA) under Education Law §6902(3)(b-1). Even with FPA, must follow clinic-specific procedural policies for aesthetics.
nurse_practitioner_np.notes: NPs with FPA may form their own PC/PLLC. Microneedling and PDO threading may be outside NP scope — consult legal counsel.

physician_assistant_pa.supervision_required: Must have written supervisory agreement with physician on file. Physician must be accessible.
physician_assistant_pa.notes: Cannot own medical practice entity in NY.

can_perform: Execute patient-specific physician orders for injectables
registered_nurse_rn.supervision_required: Must work under patient-specific physician orders — NOT standing orders. Physician must examine patient first and issue specific orders.
registered_nurse_rn.notes: Microneedling and PDO threading are outside RN scope in NY. Cannot perform good faith exams. Cannot independently initiate treatment.

can_perform: Laser hair removal (currently unregulated in NY — anyone can perform it)
aesthetician.supervision_required: Licensed by Department of State (not Education). Legally equivalent to medical assistant.
aesthetician.notes: Cannot perform microneedling (invasive). Cannot perform injectables. Laser hair removal remains unregulated in NY as of 2026. Cannot hire licensed professionals to perform procedures on their behalf.

key_regulations: Education Law §6522 — Strict CPOM prohibition. Violations are Class E felony.
Education Law §6530(19) — Fee-splitting prohibition
NYSED Office of the Professions oversees professional entity licensing
PLLC formation requires affidavit filing with NYSED (adds weeks to formation timeline)
MSO fee arrangements must be fixed and reasonable — cannot be based on volume or value of business
2026: New LLC beneficial ownership disclosure requirements
2026: DOH 30-day pre-closing notice required for material transactions (sales, PE investments, MSO restructuring)
2026: 223 businesses inspected, 87 citations issued — enforcement increasing
Energy-based device treatments (except laser hair removal) classified as medical practice as of March 2025

entity_formation.physician_entity: PC or PLLC filed with NY Department of State AND authorized by NYSED
entity_formation.mso_entity: LLC filed with NY Department of State
entity_formation.dba_filing: Assumed Name filing with county clerk
entity_formation.estimated_timeline: 8-12 weeks (PLLC requires NYSED Education Department approval which adds significant time)
key_filings: PLLC/PC Articles filed with NY DOS
entity_formation.nyc_specific: NYC may have additional local licensing requirements and zoning considerations

recent_developments: 2026: LLC Beneficial Ownership Reporting — new LLCs must file within 30 days; existing LLCs by Jan 1, 2027
2026: DOH 30-day pre-closing notice for material transactions
Energy-based devices (lasers, RF, IPL) reclassified as medical practice (March 2025) — except laser hair removal
Potential esthetician scope expansion legislation pending — monitor
Four NYC med spas closed in 2025 due to documentation lapses — enforcement is real

illinois.name: Illinois
illinois.cpom_description: Illinois does NOT strictly enforce the Corporate Practice of Medicine doctrine. Illinois is considered one of the more permissive states for med spa ownership. Non-physicians can own medical practices, though a licensed physician medical director must oversee all medical services.

ownership.non_physician_path: Non-physicians can own a med spa directly through an LLC or corporation. An MSO structure is not legally required but may be used for business structuring and liability purposes. Must have physician medical director for all medical services.
ownership.key_statute: Medical Practice Act (225 ILCS 60); Illinois Nurse Practice Act

medical_director.who_qualifies: Licensed physician (MD or DO) with active Illinois medical license
medical_director.supervision_level: Must provide oversight for all medical procedures. Written collaborative agreements required for NPs and PAs.
medical_director.geographic_restriction: No strict geographic requirement but must be reasonably available for consultation and oversight
key_duties: Oversee all medical procedures and clinical operations
Establish treatment protocols
Supervise mid-level providers under collaborative agreements
Ensure compliance with state medical practice regulations
Review and approve treatment plans

nurse_practitioner_np.supervision_required: Illinois grants Full Practice Authority (FPA) to NPs. NPs can practice independently after meeting requirements. Written collaborative agreement may still be required depending on employment setting.
nurse_practitioner_np.notes: Illinois is a Full Practice Authority state for NPs. NPs can own med spas directly.

physician_assistant_pa.supervision_required: Must have written collaborative agreement with supervising physician.
physician_assistant_pa.notes: PA scope in aesthetics is broad under proper collaborative agreement.

can_perform: Injectables under physician orders and supervision
registered_nurse_rn.supervision_required: Must work under physician orders. Cannot independently initiate treatment.
registered_nurse_rn.notes: Scope similar to other states — orders required before performing medical procedures.

can_perform: Some laser treatments with proper training and certification
aesthetician.supervision_required: Limited to licensed scope. Medical procedures require physician delegation.
aesthetician.notes: Illinois aesthetician scope may include some device-based treatments with proper licensing.

key_regulations: No strict CPOM — non-physicians can own med spas directly
Medical Practice Act (225 ILCS 60) governs medical practice
NPs have Full Practice Authority in Illinois
Collaborative agreements still recommended even where not strictly required
Standard HIPAA, OSHA, and good faith exam requirements apply
Advertising must be truthful and not misleading

entity_formation.physician_entity: LLC, SC, PC, or Corporation filed with Illinois Secretary of State
entity_formation.mso_entity: Not required — but may be used for business structuring
entity_formation.dba_filing: Assumed Business Name registration with county clerk

recent_developments: Illinois remains one of the more business-friendly states for med spa formation
NP Full Practice Authority well-established — attracts NP-owned practices
Growing market in Chicago metro area

required_documents: Good Faith Exam / Patient Intake Form
neurotoxins_botox.staff_requirements: Must be performed by licensed physician, NP, PA, or RN under physician orders (state-dependent)

dermal_fillers.display_name: Dermal Fillers (Juvederm, Restylane, Sculptra, Radiesse, RHA, Belotero)
required_documents: Good Faith Exam / Patient Intake Form
dermal_fillers.staff_requirements: Must be performed by licensed physician, NP, PA, or RN under physician orders (state-dependent)

required_documents: Good Faith Exam / Patient Intake Form
iv_therapy.staff_requirements: Must be administered by RN or above under physician protocols. TX HB 3749 has specific supervision requirements.

required_documents: Good Faith Exam / Patient Intake Form
microneedling.staff_requirements: Classified as medical procedure in most states. Cannot be performed by aestheticians in CA and NY. Requires physician delegation.

laser_treatments.display_name: Laser & Energy-Based Treatments (IPL, RF Microneedling, Fractional Laser, Sciton, Morpheus8)
required_documents: Good Faith Exam / Patient Intake Form
laser_treatments.staff_requirements: Classified as medical practice in NY (as of March 2025 except hair removal). Must be performed by qualified licensed provider. Device-specific training required.

required_documents: Weight Loss / GLP-1 Treatment Consent Form
Comprehensive Medical History & Physical Exam Form
compliance_documents: FDA Compounding Compliance (if using compounded medications)
weight_loss_glp1.staff_requirements: Prescriptive authority required. Must be prescribed by physician, NP, or PA. Monitor FDA/Eli Lilly regulatory developments for compounded tirzepatide.

required_documents: Good Faith Exam / Patient Intake Form
chemical_peels.staff_requirements: Superficial peels may be performed by aestheticians in most states. Medium and deep peels are medical procedures requiring physician or mid-level provider.

prp_therapy.display_name: PRP Therapy (Hair Loss, Facial Rejuvenation, Joint Therapy)
required_documents: Good Faith Exam / Patient Intake Form
prp_therapy.staff_requirements: Blood draw and PRP preparation/injection is a medical procedure. Requires physician, NP, PA, or RN under physician orders.

hormone_therapy.display_name: Hormone Replacement Therapy (TRT, Estradiol, Pellet Therapy)
hormone_therapy.staff_requirements: Requires prescriptive authority. Must be managed by physician, NP, or PA. Lab monitoring required.

body_contouring.display_name: Body Contouring (CoolSculpting, RF Body Tightening, Kybella)
required_documents: Good Faith Exam / Patient Intake Form
body_contouring.staff_requirements: Medical procedure requiring physician delegation. Device-specific training and certification required.

documents: Patient Consent to Use & Disclose PHI
Patient Right to Access Records Policy
Patient Consent to Leave Detailed Message

management_services_agreement.description: The core document connecting the MSO and the physician-owned professional entity. Defines business services provided by the MSO, payment terms, and ensures clinical independence of the medical practice.
management_services_agreement.when_needed: Required in all MSO structures (TX, CA, NY, and any state where non-physician needs MSO model)

medical_director_agreement.description: Establishes the relationship between the med spa and its medical director. Defines duties, compensation, supervision requirements, and compliance obligations.
medical_director_agreement.when_needed: Required for every med spa in every state

employment_agreement.description: Employment contract for clinical staff. Includes MSO-specific provisions where applicable. Covers scope of duties, compensation, supervision, non-compete, and IP.

independent_contractor_agreement.description: 1099 contractor agreement for clinical providers. Establishes independent contractor relationship, avoids employee misclassification. MSO version includes three-party structure.
independent_contractor_agreement.when_needed: When hiring clinical providers as independent contractors (1099)

membership_agreement.description: Membership contract for patients subscribing to treatment packages or wellness plans. Covers terms, payments, cancellation, liability.
membership_agreement.when_needed: When offering membership or package-based pricing to patients

nda_mutual.description: Protects confidential information shared between parties. Used for partnerships, vendor relationships, potential acquisitions.
nda_mutual.when_needed: Before sharing confidential business information with potential partners, investors, or vendors

phase_1_intake.name: Initial Intake & Assessment
phase_1_intake.description: Gather client information, determine state requirements, recommend entity structure and membership tier

phase_2_formation.name: Entity Formation
phase_2_formation.description: Form the correct business entities, file with state, obtain EIN, set up MSO if needed

phase_3_medical_director.name: Medical Director Setup
phase_3_medical_director.description: Find and onboard medical director, draft and execute agreement

phase_4_compliance.name: Compliance Package Assembly
phase_4_compliance.description: Assemble all consent forms, HIPAA/OSHA documents, policies, and protocols based on services offered

phase_5_staff_contracts.name: Staff Contracts & Onboarding
phase_5_staff_contracts.description: Draft employment/IC agreements for all clinical and non-clinical staff
